key_points: Light is a form of energy that enables us to see objects
Objects are visible when light reflects from them and enters our eyes
Reflection of light occurs when light bounces off a surface
A mirror is a smooth surface that reflects light and forms images
Lenses are transparent objects that bend light through refraction
examples: Seeing your face in a mirror due to reflection of light
Using a magnifying glass to enlarge small objects
Rear-view mirrors in vehicles helping drivers see behind
Spectacles using lenses to correct vision
formulas: Reflection of light → Light strikes a surface and bounces back
Law of reflection → Angle of incidence = Angle of reflection
Refraction → Bending of light when it passes from one medium to another
exam_tips: Know the difference between mirrors and lenses
Use examples like mirrors and spectacles when explaining concepts
Practice drawing simple ray diagrams for mirrors
